results.question: What is the language that is used by the largest number of Asian nations?

LIMIT 1;

WHERE c.Continent = 'Asia'

ORDER BY CountryCount DESC
LIMIT 1;

WHERE c.Continent = 'Asia'

LIMIT 1;

results.question: Which are the car makers which produce at least 2 models and more than 3 car makers ? List the id and the maker .
results.gold_sql: select t1.id ,  t1.maker from car_makers as t1 join model_list as t2 on t1.id  =  t2.maker group by t1.id having count(*)  >=  2 intersect select t1.id ,  t1.maker from car_makers as t1 join model_list as t2 on t1.id  =  t2.maker join car_names as t3 on t2.model  =  t3.model group by t1.id having count(*)  >  3;

S1.sql:
SELECT Id, Maker 
FROM car_makers 
WHERE Id IN (
    SELECT Maker 
    FROM model_list 
    GROUP BY Maker 
    HAVING COUNT(ModelId) >= 2
) 
GROUP BY Id 
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT Maker) > 3;

S2.sql:
SELECT ml.Maker, cm.Maker
FROM model_list ml
JOIN car_makers cm ON ml.Maker = cm.Id
GROUP BY ml.Maker
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ml.Model) >= 2
AND COUNT(DISTINCT ml.Maker) > 3;

S3.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT ml.Maker, cm.Id
FROM model_list ml
JOIN car_makers cm ON ml.Maker = cm.Id
WHERE ml.Maker IN (
    SELECT Maker
    FROM model_list
    GROUP BY Maker
    HAVING COUNT(Model) >= 2
) AND
    (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Maker) FROM model_list) > 3;

results.question: What are the ids and names of all countries that either have more than 3 car makers or produce fiat model ?
results.gold_sql: select t1.countryid ,  t1.countryname from countries as t1 join car_makers as t2 on t1.countryid  =  t2.country group by t1.countryid having count(*)  >  3 union select t1.countryid ,  t1.countryname from countries as t1 join car_makers as t2 on t1.countryid  =  t2.country join model_list as t3 on t2.id  =  t3.maker where t3.model  =  'fiat';

S1.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.CountryId, c.CountryName 
FROM countries c 
LEFT JOIN car_makers cm ON c.CountryName = cm.Country 
LEFT JOIN car_names cn ON cm.Id = cn.MakeId 
WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM car_makers WHERE Country = c.CountryName) > 3 
   OR cn.Model = 'fiat';

S2.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.CountryId, c.CountryName
FROM countries c
LEFT JOIN car_makers cm ON c.CountryId = cm.Country
LEFT JOIN model_list ml ON cm.Id = ml.Maker
LEFT JOIN car_names cn ON ml.Model = cn.Model
WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM car_makers WHERE Country = c.CountryId) > 3
   OR cn.Make = 'fiat';

S3.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.CountryId, c.CountryName 
FROM countries c

results.question: Which airlines have departing flights from both APG and CVO airports?

WHERE f.SourceAirport IN ('APG', 'CVO')
GROUP BY a.Airline
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT f.SourceAirport) = 2;

results.question: What is the name of the visitor who visited both a museum opened before 2009 and a museum opened after 2011?
results.gold_sql: SELECT t1.name FROM visitor AS t1 JOIN visit AS t2 ON t1.id  =  t2.visitor_id JOIN museum AS t3 ON t3.Museum_ID  =  t2.Museum_ID WHERE t3.open_year  <  2009 INTERSECT SELECT t1.name FROM visitor AS t1 JOIN visit AS t2 ON t1.id  =  t2.visitor_id JOIN museum AS t3 ON t3.Museum_ID  =  t2.Museum_ID WHERE t3.open_year  >  2011

S1.sql:
SELECT v.Name 
FROM visitor v 
JOIN visit vi1 ON v.ID = vi1.visitor_ID 
JOIN museum m1 ON vi1.Museum_ID = m1.Museum_ID 
JOIN visit vi2 ON v.ID = vi2.visitor_ID 
JOIN museum m2 ON vi2.Museum_ID = m2.Museum_ID 
WHERE m1.Open_Year < 2009 AND m2.Open_Year > 2011 
GROUP BY v.ID 
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT m1.Museum_ID) > 0 AND COUNT(DISTINCT m2.Museum_ID) > 0;

S2.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT v.Name
FROM visitor v
JOIN visit vi1 ON v.ID = vi1.visitor_ID
JOIN museum m1 ON vi1.Museum_ID = m1.Museum_ID
JOIN visit vi2 ON v.ID = vi2.visitor_ID
JOIN museum m2 ON vi2.Museum_ID = m2.Museum_ID
WHERE m1.Open_Year < '2009' AND m2.Open_Year > '2011';

S3.sql:
SELECT v.Name 
FROM visitor v 
JOIN visit vi1 ON v.ID = vi1.visitor_ID 
JOIN museum m1 ON vi1.Museum_ID = m1.Museum_ID 
JOIN visit vi2 ON v.ID = vi2.visitor_ID 
JOIN museum m2 ON vi2.Museum_ID = m2.Museum_ID 
WHERE m1.Open_Year < '2009' AND m2.Open_Year > '2011';

results.question: What are the id and names of the countries which have more than 3 car makers or produce the 'fiat' model?
results.gold_sql: SELECT T1.countryId ,  T1.CountryName FROM Countries AS T1 JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T2 ON T1.CountryId  =  T2.Country GROUP BY T1.countryId HAVING count(*)  >  3 UNION SELECT T1.countryId ,  T1.CountryName FROM Countries AS T1 JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T2 ON T1.CountryId  =  T2.Country JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T3 ON T2.Id  =  T3.Maker WHERE T3.Model  =  'fiat';

S1.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.CountryId, c.CountryName 
FROM countries c 
LEFT JOIN car_makers cm ON c.CountryName = cm.Country 
LEFT JOIN model_list ml ON cm.Maker = ml.Maker 
WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM car_makers WHERE Country = c.CountryName) > 3 
   OR ml.Model = 'fiat';

S2.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.CountryId, c.CountryName
FROM countries c
LEFT JOIN car_makers cm ON c.CountryId = cm.Country
LEFT JOIN model_list ml ON cm.Id = ml.Maker
LEFT JOIN car_names cn ON ml.Model = cn.Model
WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM car_makers WHERE Country = c.CountryId) > 3
   OR cn.Make = 'fiat';

S3.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT CountryId, CountryName 
FROM ( 
    SELECT c.CountryId, c.CountryName 
    FROM countries c 
    JOIN car_makers cm ON c.CountryId = cm.Country 
    GROUP BY c.CountryId, c.CountryName 
    HAVING COUNT(cm.Id) > 3 
    UNION 
    SELECT c.CountryId, c.CountryName 
    FROM countries c 
    JOIN car_makers cm ON c.CountryId = cm.Country 
    JOIN model_list ml ON cm.Id = ml.Maker 
    WHERE ml.Model = 'fiat' 
) AS combined;

results.question: What are the ids and makers of all car makers that produce at least 2 models and make more than 3 cars?
results.gold_sql: SELECT T1.Id ,  T1.Maker FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.Maker GROUP BY T1.Id HAVING count(*)  >=  2 INTERSECT SELECT T1.Id ,  T1.Maker FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.Maker JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T3 ON T2.model  =  T3.model GROUP BY T1.Id HAVING count(*)  >  3;

S1.sql:
SELECT cm.Id, cm.Maker 
FROM car_makers cm 
JOIN model_list ml ON cm.Id = ml.Maker 
JOIN car_names cn ON ml.Model = cn.Model 
JOIN cars_data cd ON cn.MakeId = cd.Id 
GROUP BY cm.Id, cm.Maker 
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ml.Model) >= 2 AND COUNT(cd.Id) > 3;

S2.sql:
SELECT cm.Id, cm.Maker
FROM car_makers cm
JOIN model_list ml ON cm.Id = ml.Maker
JOIN car_names cn ON ml.Model = cn.Model
JOIN cars_data cd ON cn.MakeId = cd.Id
GROUP BY cm.Id, cm.Maker
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ml.Model) >= 2 AND COUNT(cd.Id) > 3;

S3.sql:
SELECT car_makers.Id, car_makers.Maker
FROM car_makers
JOIN model_list ON car_makers.Id = model_list.Maker
WHERE car_makers.Id IN (SELECT MakeId
FROM cars_data
JOIN car_names ON cars_data.Id = car_names.MakeId
GROUP BY Make
HAVING COUNT(*) > 3)
GROUP BY car_makers.Id
HAVING COUNT(model_list.ModelId) >= 2;

results.question: Find the id, last name and cell phone of the professionals who live in the state of Indiana or have performed more than two treatments.
results.gold_sql: SELECT professional_id ,  last_name ,  cell_number FROM Professionals WHERE state  =  'Indiana' UNION SELECT T1.professional_id ,  T1.last_name ,  T1.cell_number FROM Professionals AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.professional_id  =  T2.professional_id GROUP BY T1.professional_id HAVING count(*)  >  2

S1.sql: SELECT professional_id, last_name, cell_number FROM Professionals WHERE state = 'Indiana' OR professional_id IN (SELECT professional_id FROM Treatments GROUP BY professional_id HAVING COUNT(treatment_id) > 2);

S2.sql:
SELECT professional_id, last_name, cell_number 
FROM Professionals 
WHERE state = 'Indiana' 
   OR professional_id IN (SELECT professional_id 
                          FROM Treatments 
                          GROUP BY professional_id 
                          HAVING COUNT(treatment_id) > 2);

S3.sql: SELECT professional_id, last_name, cell_number FROM Professionals WHERE state = 'Indiana' UNION SELECT DISTINCT p.professional_id, p.last_name, p.cell_number FROM Professionals p JOIN Treatments t ON p.professional_id = t.professional_id GROUP BY p.professional_id HAVING COUNT(t.treatment_id) > 2;

results.question: Show the years in which orchestras that have given more than one performance are founded.
results.gold_sql: SELECT Year_of_Founded FROM orchestra AS T1 JOIN performance AS T2 ON T1.Orchestra_ID  =  T2.Orchestra_ID GROUP BY T2.Orchestra_ID HAVING COUNT(*)  >  1

S1.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT o.Year_of_Founded
FROM orchestra o
JOIN performance p ON o.Orchestra_ID = p.Orchestra_ID
GROUP BY o.Orchestra_ID
HAVING COUNT(p.Performance_ID) > 1;

S2.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT o.Year_of_Founded
FROM orchestra o
JOIN performance p ON o.Orchestra_ID = p.Orchestra_ID
GROUP BY o.Orchestra_ID
HAVING COUNT(p.Performance_ID) > 1;

S3.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT o.Year_of_Founded 
FROM orchestra o 
JOIN performance p ON o.Orchestra_ID = p.Orchestra_ID 
JOIN (SELECT Orchestra_ID 
      FROM performance 
      GROUP BY Orchestra_ID 
      HAVING COUNT(Performance_ID) > 1) AS multiple_performances 
ON p.Orchestra_ID = multiple_performances.Orchestra_ID;

results.question: Show the stadium name and capacity with most number of concerts in year 2014 or after.

results.question: What is the first, middle, and last name, along with the id and number of enrollments, for the student who enrolled the most in any program?

results.question: What are the names of the dogs for which the owner has not spend more than 1000 for treatment ?
results.gold_sql: select name from dogs where dog_id not in ( select dog_id from treatments group by dog_id having sum(cost_of_treatment)  >  1000 )

S1.sql:
SELECT d.name 
FROM Dogs d 
JOIN Owners o ON d.owner_id = o.owner_id 
LEFT JOIN Treatments t ON d.dog_id = t.dog_id 
GROUP BY d.dog_id 
HAVING COALESCE(SUM(t.cost_of_treatment), 0) <= 1000;

S2.sql:
SELECT D.name 
FROM Dogs D 
JOIN Treatments T ON D.dog_id = T.dog_id 
GROUP BY D.dog_id 
HAVING SUM(T.cost_of_treatment) <= 1000;

S3.sql:
SELECT D.name 
FROM Dogs D 
JOIN Treatments T ON D.dog_id = T.dog_id 
GROUP BY D.dog_id 
HAVING SUM(T.cost_of_treatment) <= 1000;

results.question: What are the students' first names who have both cats and dogs as pets?
results.gold_sql: SELECT T1.Fname FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN pets AS T3 ON T3.petid  =  T2.petid WHERE T3.pettype  =  'cat' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Fname FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN pets AS T3 ON T3.petid  =  T2.petid WHERE T3.pettype  =  'dog'

S1.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT S.Fname 
FROM Student S 
JOIN Has_Pet HP ON S.StuID = HP.StuID 
JOIN Pets P ON HP.PetID = P.PetID 
WHERE P.PetType IN ('cat', 'dog') 
GROUP BY S.StuID 
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT P.PetType) = 2;

S2.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT s.Fname 
FROM Student s 
JOIN Has_Pet hp ON s.StuID = hp.StuID 
JOIN Pets p ON hp.PetID = p.PetID 
WHERE p.PetType IN ('cat', 'dog') 
GROUP BY s.StuID 
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.PetType) = 2;

S3.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT s.Fname 
FROM Student s 
JOIN Has_Pet hp ON s.StuID = hp.StuID 
JOIN Pets p ON hp.PetID = p.PetID 
WHERE p.PetType IN ('cat', 'dog') 
GROUP BY s.StuID 
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.PetType) = 2;
